segments.s157-g17: کهن‌گشته is a participle/adjective meaning ‘grown old’ placed before the noun for poetic emphasis. این داستانها is the noun phrase ‘these tales’; ز من is a prepositional phrase ‘by/from me’ indicating the tales are ones the speaker told (or that have aged through his telling). Word order is topicalized: participle + demonstrative + noun + agent.
segments.s157-g18: همی is a present/continuous particle here giving an ongoing or emphatic sense (‘now/still’). نو is an adjective ‘new’ and شود is the 3rd-person singular present of شدن ‘become/be made’; together نو شود = ‘is/are made new’. بر سر انجمن is an adverbial locative phrase ‘before/in the assembly’ (literally ‘upon the head of the assembly’), placed after the verb as the scene of renewal.
segments.s157-g19: اگر introduces a counterfactual conditional ‘if’. زندگانی is the noun ‘life’. بود is the past-tense form of بودن used here to express the hypothetical (‘were’). دیریاز is an adjective/adverb meaning ‘long-standing/long’ and functions as the predicate of the conditional clause.
segments.s157-g20: برین دین is a locative phrase: بر + ین (orthographic variant of 'این') + دین = ‘in/upon this religion/faith’. خرم is an adjective ‘joyful, happy’ modifying the implied subject (I). بمانم is the 1st-person singular subjunctive/potential of ماندن ‘to remain’ used as the apodosis after the conditional ‘if...’ (so: ‘I would remain’). دراز is an adverb/adjectival complement ‘for a long time’. Word order places the locative and descriptive elements before the verb bمانم.
segments.s157-g21: یکی here means ‘a certain/one (of them)’. میوه‌داری (literally ‘fruit-having’) is an adjective ‘fruit-bearing’ (i.e., a tree or plant that bears fruit). بماند is 3rd-person singular present/subjunctive of ماندن ‘will remain / remains’. ز من ‘of/from me’ marks possession or origin (‘one of mine will remain’).
segments.s157-g22: که introduces a relative clause ‘that/which’. بارد here functions as a verb meaning ‘bears/yields/lets fall’ (an older/poetic usage related to باریدن/بار دادن) and governs بار ‘load/produce’ (the tree’s fruit). همی gives a continuous/emphatic sense ‘always/still’. او is the pronoun ‘it/its’ (referring to the tree), and بر چمن is the locative ‘upon the meadow/grass’. Note: the printed senses for بارد in the word-list are for ‘cold’, but in this context بارد is an archaic/poetic verbal form meaning ‘bears/yields’ (sense not listed), so that entry is marked accordingly.
segments.s157-g23: ازان پس is an adverbial phrase ‘after that/thereupon’. که introduces the subordinate clause ‘when/that’, and بنمود is a past-tense verb here meaning ‘he completed/made appear’ (in context: ‘he completed’ or ‘reached’ the age). پنچاه و هشت is the compound numeral ‘fifty-eight’. Overall this segment sets the temporal point: ‘after that, when fifty-eight was reached’.
segments.s157-g24: بسر here is part of the verbal idiom بسر گذشت ‘to pass (time)’, with گذشت as the main verb at the end. بر فراوان شگفتی functions as an adverbial phrase ‘with abundant wonder’ or ‘in great wonder’. The verb گذشت (past tense) completes the clause: ‘(time) passed in abundant wonder’ or ‘many wonders occurred/passed by.’ Word order is typical for Persian poetry: modifiers precede the final verb.
segments.s157-g25: همی is an adverb meaning 'still/continually' placed before the noun for emphasis. آز is the noun subject 'greed'; کمتر نگردد is a negated present/subjunctive-like predicate ('does not become less' / 'is not diminished') with the adjective کمتر placed before the verb (typical classical word order). بسال is an adverbial phrase 'in the year' placed after the verb to mark duration.
segments.s157-g26: همی again marks continuous action ('continually'). روز is the object/thing sought and is placed before the verb جوید (classical order: object then verb); جوید is a present tense verb 'seeks'. بتقویم and فال are instrumental/measure phrases introduced by the prefix بَـ (here written as part of the word) meaning 'by/according to' and are joined by و 'and' to show the means of seeking.
segments.s157-g27: چه is the interrogative 'what' fronted for the question. گفته‌ست is the perfect/periphrastic verb 'has said' (the classical contracted form of گفته است). آن موبد پیش‌رو is the subject phrase 'that foremost mobad', with پیش‌رو functioning as a postpositive adjective 'foremost/leading'. Note the classical order: interrogative + verb + subject.
segments.s157-g28: که introduces the reported content clause ('that'). هرگز is the negative adverb 'never'. نگردد is the classical present/subjunctive form 'becomes' with negation (here used to mean 'will never become' / 'never turns into'). کهن‌گشته is a past-participial/adjectival form 'having become old' (literally 'old-made'), and نو is the predicative adjective 'new' — together meaning 'that which has become old will not become new.'
segments.s157-g29: تو چندان که گویی is an idiomatic protasis meaning 'to the extent that you say' or 'as much as you claim.' چندان ... که introduces the measure ('so much that'), and گویی here functions as 'you say' (classical present) — the line then issues the imperative سخن‌گوی باش ('be a speaker' / 'be eloquent'): سخن‌گوی is a noun/adjective 'speaker/eloquent' and باش is the imperative 'be'.
segments.s157-g30: This is a string of imperatives: خردمند باش ('be wise') then و linking to جهانجوی باش ('and be a seeker of the world' / 'be ambitious'). خردمند and جهانجوی are predicate adjectives/nouns; each is followed by the imperative باش. The repetition of باش is a typical poetic insistence on each quality.
segments.s157-g31: چو رفتی is a temporal clause 'when/after you go' (چو = 'when'). رفتی is the second-person past/punctual form 'you went/you leave' used in the temporal sense. سر و کار is an idiomatic compound 'head and work' meaning 'affairs/business', and با ایزدست means 'is with God' / 'rests with God' — ایزدست here is an archaic fused form (ایزد است) indicating that the matter belongs to or depends on God.
segments.s157-g32: اگر introduces another conditional 'if'. نیک is 'good/auspicious' describing جای 'place/position'. باشدت is an archaic subjunctive/jussive form (باشد + enclitic ت) meaning 'be to you' i.e. 'be for you' ('may it be' / 'if it be'). ار is a second conditional/continuative particle ('then' / 'if'), and بدست literally 'by/through the hand' is instrumental, here elliptically referring back to God (i.e. 'by the hand [of God]').
segments.s157-g33: نگر is an imperative 'look / see' addressing the listener. تا introduces an indirect clause (here: 'to see what...'); چه is the interrogative 'what' and کاری (here 'an action, deed') is the noun being asked about. همان ('the same') refers forward to what will follow; بدروی is a verbal/adjectival form meaning something like 'you will meet/receive in return' or 'the same will be done to you' — the whole small clause yields 'see what action — the same will be done to you.'
segments.s157-g34: سخن 'speech / words' is the topic, modified by the relative phrase هرچه گویی 'whatever (you) say' (هرچه = 'whatever'; گویی is the 2nd-person present/subjunctive 'you say' used as a general statement). همان بشنوی 'you will hear the same' uses همان to pick up 'سخن' and بشنوی (2nd-person present/subjunctive/indicative in older style) to state the result: what you say you will likewise hear.
segments.s157-g35: درشتی 'harshness / rudeness' is the subject; ز کس نشنود is a negative clause with ز = 'from' and کس = 'anyone', and نشنود is an older 3rd‑person present negative form 'does not hear / does not heed'. The following نرم‌گوی is an imperative 'speak softly / be soft-spoken' directed to the addressee, so the clause reads roughly 'Harshness heeds no one; (so) speak gently.'
segments.s157-g36: به جز 'except' frames the restriction: به = 'in / with / regarding', جز = 'except', نیکوی = 'the good/virtuous (one)'. در زمانه is a locative phrase 'in this age/age/time' and مجوی is a negative imperative 'do not seek' (classical negative command). Together: 'Do not seek (anyone) in this age except the virtuous.'
segments.s157-g37: به گفتار دهقان is a prepositional phrase 'to / regarding the speech of the farmer' (به + گفتار). کنون 'now' is an adverb of time. بازگرد is an imperative 'return' or 'turn back' directed at the listener: 'Return now to the farmer's speech.'
segments.s157-g38: نگر again is an imperative 'look / consider'. تا introduces the indirect interrogative clause 'to see what...' (تا + چه = 'what'), and گوید is 3rd‑person singular present 'he says' (classical form); سراینده مرد is a noun phrase 'the composing man / poet' (سراینده = poet/songster, مرد = man), so the whole clause means 'See what the poet-man says.'
segments.s157-g1: کنون‌ای is a vocative-like opening ('now, O ...') addressing someone; سخن‌گوی is a noun in apposition (address) meaning a person who speaks or is eloquent, and بیدارمغز is a compound adjective 'quick‑witted / clear‑headed' modifying that addressee. The particle ای marks the vocative; word order is address (adverb + vocative noun + adjectival epithet).
segments.s157-g2: یکی داستانی is an indefinite noun phrase 'a certain tale' (یکی = 'a/one', داستانی = 'tale' with the adjectival/noun‑forming suffix). بیارای is an imperative/form of آراییدن 'adorn/compose' (here a poetical command 'compose/adorn'); نغز is a postpositive qualifier meaning 'witty/delightful' and qualifies how the tale should be made. Note the older Persian word order: object/phrase before the verb.
segments.s157-g3: چون introduces a subordinate clause ('when' / 'as'); سخن is the subject 'speech/words'; برابر شود is a verb phrase 'become equal / be matched' (شود = become/be); با خرد is a prepositional complement 'with wisdom'. Together: 'when speech is in accord with wisdom...'.
segments.s157-g4: روان سراینده is a compact possessive expression 'the poet's spirit' (poetic placement without an explicit ezāfe); رامش برد is a verbal phrase 'takes/gains repose' (بردن used here as 'to obtain/take', with برد as the finite verb). The effect is: the poet's spirit obtains peace when speech accords with wisdom.
segments.s157-g5: کسی را که ... بود is a common classical construction 'someone who ... was'; را here marks the topical/affected person; که introduces the relative clause 'who'; اندیشه ناخوش بود = 'his thought was unhappy/unsettled' (بود as past copula).
segments.s157-g6: بدان ناخوشی is an adverbial/relative phrase 'in/through that displeasure' (ب = to/into/prefix + دان here representing the demonstrative element), رای او = 'his counsel/decision', and گش بود = 'was loose/relaxed/unstable' (گش is an archaic adjective meaning open/loose; بود is the copula). The clause means that the ill mood made his judgment lax or unreliable.
segments.s157-g7: همی is an adverb meaning 'continually / constantly'; خویشتن را is the object 'himself' (را marking the object/emphasis); چلیپا کند is a transitive verb phrase 'makes (into) a cross' (چلیپا = 'cross', کند = 'makes/does'). The clause describes a habitual self‑degrading action (metaphorical).
segments.s157-g8: به پیش خردمند is a prepositional phrase 'in the presence of / before the wise' (به پیش = 'before/in front of', خردمند = 'the wise one'); رسوا کند repeats the verb pattern 'renders/ makes (him) disgraced' (رسوا = 'disgraced', کند = 'does/makes'). The verb again is habitual; the whole clause means he brings shame upon himself before the wise.
segments.s157-g9: ولیکن is an adversative conjunction ('but') introducing a contrast. نبیند is the present form with the negative prefix ن- ('does not see' / 'sees not'); the verb appears before کس (an indefinite pronoun) by poetic inversion, yielding 'no one sees...' آهوی خویش is the object phrase: آهوی (gazelle) + خویش split into خوی + ش, where ش is the third‑person possessive suffix ('his/her/its'); خوی here functions as a substantive meaning 'own/nature' or 'property' attached to the possessed noun.
segments.s157-g10: ترا is the dative/benefactive form written as ت + را ('to you' / 'for you') placed before the predicate; روشن آید is a light verb construction meaning 'becomes clear' (روشن = clear/evident, آید = 'comes/becomes'). همه خوی خویش uses a doubled خوی (a poetic intensifier) with the final ش as third‑person possessive, i.e. 'all of his own disposition/ways'; the phrase as a whole means 'to you all his nature/ways become apparent.'
segments.s157-g11: اگر introduces a conditional ('if'). داد is the abstract noun 'justice' (not a past tense of 'give' here). باید is impersonal/modal 'it must/it is necessary', and که introduces the subordinate clause 'that it remain'; مانَد is the present subjunctive/optative sense 'remain/continue' (here equivalent to 'remain in force'), and بجای is an adverbial phrase 'in place'/'in its place'. The whole segment = 'If justice must remain in its place...'.
segments.s157-g12: بیارای functions adverbially/qualitatively here ('unadorned' / 'plainly' / 'without ornament') modifying the following command; ازین پس is the time phrase 'from this after' = 'from now on' (از + این = 'from this', پس = 'after/ henceforth'). بدانا is dative/benefactive 'to the wise' (بـ + دانا), and نمای is an imperative or jussive 'show/display/tell'. Together the clause issues: 'then, from now on present it plainly and show it to the wise.'
segments.s157-g13: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when'. دانا ('the wise') is the subject that precedes the verb by poetic order. پسندد is the present/potential verb 'approves/likes', and پسندیده گشت is a resultative phrase: پسندیده (approved/pleasing) + گشت ('became'), i.e. 'it became approved.' The clause is a temporal/result statement: 'When the wise approve, it becomes approved.'
segments.s157-g14: به جوی تو در is a prepositional locative phrase with inverted word order: به ('in/to'), جوی ('stream'), تو ('your') and در ('in/within')—poetic placement puts the elements out of the modern order but yields 'in your stream'. آب is the subject 'the water'. چون دیده گشت is a simile/result: چون ('like/as') + دیده (past participle 'seen' or metaphorically 'an eye') + گشت ('became'), i.e. 'became like an eye' or 'became seen/visible like an eye.' The clause describes a change of state of the water in your stream.
segments.s157-g15: ز is the poetic preposition 'from'. گفتار دهقان is a genitive/attributive phrase 'the speech/words of the peasant' (دهقان = peasant/farmer). کنون is the temporal adverb 'now'. داستان is the noun 'story/tale' and here is the object/topic to be presented. The sequence yields 'From the peasant's words, now (the) tale ...'.
segments.s157-g16: تو is the vocative/subject 'you' (addressing the teller). برخوان is an imperative/formulaic phrase 'recite at the table' or 'set before (the assembly)' (بر- + خوان), and و coordinates the next imperative. برگوی is the imperative of گفتن with the prefix بر- 'tell/relate'. با راستان is a prepositional phrase 'with/by the righteous' or 'to the upright' (با = with, راستان = the righteous/upright); together the commands read 'you recite it at the table and tell it with/to the righteous.'

sentences.s157-s9: These tales, grown old by me, are now made new before the assembly.
sentences.s157-s10: If life were long, I would remain happily in this faith for a long time.
sentences.s157-s11: One fruit-bearing (tree) will remain of mine, so that its load (fruit) will always fall upon the meadow.
sentences.s157-s12: After that, when he had completed fifty-eight (years), many wonders passed.
sentences.s157-s13: Greed does not grow less through the year; it continually seeks a day by calendar and omen.
sentences.s157-s14: What has that foremost mobad (priest) said — that what is old never becomes new?
sentences.s157-s15: Be as much as you say: be a speaker; be wise and be a seeker of the world.
sentences.s157-s16: When you go, your affairs are with God; if the place be auspicious for you, it is (so) by (His) hand.
sentences.s157-s17: See what deed is done — you will receive the same; whatever words you say, you will hear the same.
sentences.s157-s18: Harshness listens to no one; speak softly — seek in this age none but the good.
sentences.s157-s19: Return now to the farmer's words; see what the composing man (the poet) says.
sentences.s157-s1: Now, O eloquent, quick‑witted speaker, compose a delightful tale.
sentences.s157-s2: When words are matched with wisdom, the poet's spirit finds repose.
sentences.s157-s3: If anyone's thought was ill, that ill thought made his counsel lax.
sentences.s157-s4: He continually makes himself a cross and disgraces himself before the wise.
sentences.s157-s5: But no one sees his own gazelle; to you all of his nature becomes clear.
sentences.s157-s6: If justice must remain in its place, then set it forth plainly from now on and show it to the wise.
sentences.s157-s7: When the wise approve, it becomes approved; in your stream the water became like an eye.
sentences.s157-s8: Now, from the peasant's words tell your tale: you recite it at the table and relate it with the righteous.
